cost_sensitivity.cost_model: Future diagnostics must reject interpretation if any OPEX-day readout only survives before fees, slippage, and spread assumptions.
cost_sensitivity.scope: Value-free migration metadata only; no execution cost estimate is produced.

cost_assumptions.scope: research planning only; no execution or tradability claim

data_assumptions.data_dependency_detail: OPEX (third-Friday) and quad-witching (quarterly) dates are deterministic calendar rules requiring no external feed. This is the lowest-data-dependency card. Strike-level options data (open interest, pin strikes) is paid data and is explicitly OUT OF SCOPE for this card.
data_assumptions.missing_label_candidates: No new label candidates are declared by this legacy card.

exclusion_rules: Restrict the conditioner to known-ahead calendar flags and time-to-RTH-close / time-to-expiration clocks. Any strike-level or open-interest input is out of scope here (needs paid options data) and would require its own card and point-in-time guard.
Do not test needs_paid_data records in V0; paid-data sourcing is RED-deferred.
Do not edit or delete the legacy Track-A card until a later retirement phase is authorized.
expected_failure_modes: Conditioning on a deterministic options-expiration calendar is structurally distinct from the rejected realized-price/quote main effects. Because the hypothesized 'pinning' is a price-path outcome, the OUTCOME measurement may overlap with vwap_session/return_to_vwap dynamics; orthogonality of the OUTCOME must be checked, but the CONDITIONER is independent of any dead-mechanism input.

Residualize forward outcomes against vwap_session and regime factors before scoring the OPEX-day conditioner; report marginal contribution. Cross-check that OPEX flag is not merely a proxy for day_of_week or month-end already captured elsewhere.

OPEX (third-Friday) and quad-witching (quarterly) dates are deterministic calendar rules requiring no external feed. This is the lowest-data-dependency card. Strike-level options data (open interest, pin strikes) is paid data and is explicitly OUT OF SCOPE for this card.
promotion_criteria: Any future WATCH or CANDIDATE routing requires reviewer-gated evidence and must preserve EXPLORATORY promotion guards.
Any future diagnostic must pass no-lookahead, duplicate-exposure, and testability checks before interpretation.

timestamp_assumptions.lookahead_description: OPEX/quad-witching dates are fully deterministic from the exchange calendar and known years ahead, so the calendar conditioner itself carries little lookahead hazard. The hazard appears only if strike/open-interest 'pin' levels are introduced (those require point-in-time options data and could leak realized positioning).
timestamp_assumptions.lookahead_level: low lookahead risk declared by the legacy Track-A card

hypothesis_card.author: ALPHA_IDEA_TO_VERDICT_LOOP_V0/IVL-P01
hypothesis_card.expected_mechanism: On scheduled options-expiration (OPEX) and quad-witching sessions, we test whether intraday forward behavior conditioned on the known-ahead OPEX-day flag and time-to-expiration differs from non-OPEX sessions (a 'pinning'-type conditioning hypothesis). Falsifiable; not a profitability claim.
falsification_criteria: Reject or requeue this migrated record if required governed features or labels are unavailable before diagnostics.
Block any later verdict if no-lookahead controls or duplicate exposure checks fail before a metric is inspected.
hypothesis_card.rationale: Dealer hedging of expiring options positions can concentrate flow near large open-interest strikes into the expiration; whether this produces a measurable conditional forward pattern is an empirical question the diagnostics resolve. The strike/open-interest detail is NOT assumed here; the conditioner is the calendar position alone unless paid options data is approved.
risks: OPEX/quad-witching dates are fully deterministic from the exchange calendar and known years ahead, so the calendar conditioner itself carries little lookahead hazard. The hazard appears only if strike/open-interest 'pin' levels are introduced (those require point-in-time options data and could leak realized positioning).

Conditioning on a deterministic options-expiration calendar is structurally distinct from the rejected realized-price/quote main effects. Because the hypothesized 'pinning' is a price-path outcome, the OUTCOME measurement may overlap with vwap_session/return_to_vwap dynamics; orthogonality of the OUTCOME must be checked, but the CONDITIONER is independent of any dead-mechanism input.

OPEX (third-Friday) and quad-witching (quarterly) dates are deterministic calendar rules requiring no external feed. This is the lowest-data-dependency card. Strike-level options data (open interest, pin strikes) is paid data and is explicitly OUT OF SCOPE for this card.
